title: Sri Rama Ashtottara Sata Naama Stotram

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामो रामभद्रश्च रामचन्द्रश्च शाश्वतः ।
राजीवलोचनः श्रीमान्राजेन्द्रो रघुपुङ्गवः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmō rāmabhadraścha rāmachandraścha śāśvataḥ ।
rājīvalōchanaḥ śrīmānrājēndrō raghupuṅgavaḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Srirama, Ramabhadra, Ramachandra, the eternal; lotus-eyed, the glorious, king of kings, foremost of the Raghu clan.

contentBlocks.original:
जानकीवल्लभो जैत्रो जितामित्रो जनार्दनः ।
विश्वामित्रप्रियो दान्तः शरणत्राणतत्परः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jānakīvallabhō jaitrō jitāmitrō janārdanaḥ ।
viśvāmitrapriyō dāntaḥ śaraṇatrāṇatatparaḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Janaki's beloved, the victorious, conqueror of enemies, Janardana; dear to Vishvamitra, the self-controlled, ever devoted to protecting those who seek refuge.

contentBlocks.original:
वालिप्रमथनो वाग्मी सत्यवाक्सत्यविक्रमः ।
सत्यव्रतो व्रतधरः सदाहनुमदाश्रितः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vālipramathanō vāgmī satyavāksatyavikramaḥ ।
satyavratō vratadharaḥ sadāhanumadāśritaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Slayer of Vali, the eloquent, true of speech and valour; true to his vow, the upholder of vows, ever attended by Hanuman.

contentBlocks.original:
कौसलेयः खरध्वंसी विराधवधपण्डितः ।
विभीषणपरित्राता हरकोदण्डखण्डनः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kausalēyaḥ kharadhvaṃsī virādhavadhapaṇḍitaḥ ।
vibhīṣaṇaparitrātā harakōdaṇḍakhaṇḍanaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Son of Kaushalya, destroyer of Khara, skilled in slaying Viradha; deliverer of Vibhishana, shattering of Shiva's bow.

contentBlocks.original:
सप्ततालप्रभेत्ता च दशग्रीवशिरोहरः ।
जामदग्न्यमहादर्पदलनस्ताटकान्तकः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saptatālaprabhēttā cha daśagrīvaśirōharaḥ ।
jāmadagnyamahādarpadalanastāṭakāntakaḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Piercer of the seven sala trees, remover of the ten heads of Ravana; humbler of Parashurama's pride, slayer of Tataka.

contentBlocks.original:
वेदान्तसारो वेदात्मा भवरोगस्य भेषजम् ।
दूषणत्रिशिरोहन्ता त्रिमूर्तिस्त्रिगुणात्मकः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vēdāntasārō vēdātmā bhavarōgasya bhēṣajam ।
dūṣaṇatriśirōhantā trimūrtistriguṇātmakaḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Essence of the Vedanta, soul of the Vedas, medicine for samsara's disease; slayer of Dushana and Trishira, the three-formed, of three gunas.

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिविक्रमस्त्रिलोकात्मा पुण्यचारित्रकीर्तनः ।
त्रिलोकरक्षको धन्वी दण्डकारण्यकर्तनः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trivikramastrilōkātmā puṇyachāritrakīrtanaḥ ।
trilōkarakṣakō dhanvī daṇḍakāraṇyakartanaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Trivikrama, soul of the three worlds, one whose holy deeds are to be praised; protector of the three worlds, the bow-bearer, the one who cleared the Dandaka forest.

contentBlocks.original:
अहल्याशापशमनः पितृभक्तो वरप्रदः ।
जितेन्द्रियो जितक्रोधो जितामित्रो जगद्गुरुः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ahalyāśāpaśamanaḥ pitṛbhaktō varapradaḥ ।
jitēndriyō jitakrōdhō jitāmitrō jagadguruḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dispeller of Ahalya's curse, devoted to his father, granter of boons; conqueror of the senses, conqueror of anger, conqueror of foes, teacher of the universe.

contentBlocks.original:
ऋक्षवानरसङ्घाती चित्रकूटसमाश्रयः ।
जयन्तत्राणवरदः सुमित्रापुत्रसेवितः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṛkṣavānarasaṅghātī chitrakūṭasamāśrayaḥ ।
jayantatrāṇavaradaḥ sumitrāputrasēvitaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who assembled the bears and monkeys, the one who took refuge at Chitrakuta; granter of a boon to Jayanta, served by Lakshmana.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वदेवादिदेवश्च मृतवानरजीवनः ।
मायामारीचहन्ता च महादेवो महाभुजः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvadēvādidēvaścha mṛtavānarajīvanaḥ ।
māyāmārīchahantā cha mahādēvō mahābhujaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: God of all gods, reviver of the dead monkeys; slayer of the illusory Maricha, the great god of mighty arms.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वदेवस्तुतः सौम्यो ब्रह्मण्यो मुनिसंस्तुतः ।
महायोगी महोदारः सुग्रीवेप्सितराज्यदः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvadēvastutaḥ saumyō brahmaṇyō munisaṃstutaḥ ।
mahāyōgī mahōdāraḥ sugrīvēpsitarājyadaḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Praised by all gods, the gentle, devoted to Brahmins, praised by sages; the great yogi, the greatly generous, restorer of Sugriva's desired kingdom.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वपुण्याधिकफलः स्मृतसर्वाघनाशनः ।
आदिपुरुषः परमपुरुषो महापूरुष एव च ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvapuṇyādhikaphalaḥ smṛtasarvāghanāśanaḥ ।
ādipuruṣaḥ paramapuruṣō mahāpūruṣa ēva cha ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Most fruitful of all virtuous acts, destroyer of remembered sins; the primeval Purusha, the supreme Purusha, the great Purusha.

contentBlocks.original:
पुण्योदयो दयासारः पुराणपुरुषोत्तमः ।
स्मितवक्त्रो मिताभाषी पूर्वभाषी च राघवः ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
puṇyōdayō dayāsāraḥ purāṇapuruṣōttamaḥ ।
smitavaktrō mitābhāṣī pūrvabhāṣī cha rāghavaḥ ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The auspiciously risen, the ocean of compassion, the ancient Purushottama; smiling-faced, measured in speech, one who speaks first, Raghava.

contentBlocks.original:
अनन्तगुणगम्भीरो धीरोदात्तगुणोत्तमः ।
मायामानुषचारित्रो महादेवादिपूजितः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantaguṇagambhīrō dhīrōdāttaguṇōttamaḥ ।
māyāmānuṣachāritrō mahādēvādipūjitaḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of infinite virtues and depth, of heroic and noble qualities; appearing human through his divine maya, worshipped by Mahadeva and others.

contentBlocks.original:
सेतुकृज्जितवाराशिः सर्वतीर्थमयो हरिः ।
श्यामाङ्गः सुन्दरः शूरः पीतवासा धनुर्धरः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sētukṛjjitavārāśiḥ sarvatīrthamayō hariḥ ।
śyāmāṅgaḥ sundaraḥ śūraḥ pītavāsā dhanurdharaḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Builder of the bridge, conqueror of the ocean, pervading all sacred places; dark-limbed, the beautiful, the heroic, yellow-robed, bearer of the bow.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वयज्ञाधिपो यज्वा जरामरणवर्जितः ।
शिवलिङ्गप्रतिष्ठाता सर्वावगुणवर्जितः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvayajñādhipō yajvā jarāmaraṇavarjitaḥ ।
śivaliṅgapratiṣṭhātā sarvāvaguṇavarjitaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Lord of all sacrifices, the sacrificer, beyond birth and death; establisher of Shiva's linga, free from all defects.

contentBlocks.original:
परमात्मा परं ब्रह्म सच्चिदानन्दविग्रहः ।
परञ्ज्योतिः परन्धाम पराकाशः परात्परः ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paramātmā paraṃ brahma sachchidānandavigrahaḥ ।
parañjyōtiḥ parandhāma parākāśaḥ parātparaḥ ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The supreme self, the supreme Brahman, the embodiment of existence-consciousness-bliss; the supreme light, the supreme abode, the supreme sky, beyond the beyond.

contentBlocks.original: परेशः पारगः पारः सर्वदेवात्मकः परः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: parēśaḥ pāragaḥ pāraḥ sarvadēvātmakaḥ paraḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The supreme lord, the one who takes across, the shore, the essence of all the gods, the supreme.

contentBlocks.original: एवं श्रीरामचन्द्रस्य नाम्नामष्टोत्तरं शतम्
contentBlocks.transliteration: ēvaṃ śrīrāmachandrasya nāmnāmaṣṭōttaraṃ śatam
contentBlocks.translation: Thus are the 108 names of Sri Ramachandra set forth.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्री रामाष्टोत्तर शतनामस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम्
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrī rāmāṣṭōttara śatanāmastōtraṃ sampūrṇam
contentBlocks.translation: Thus the Sri Rama Ashtottara Sata Naama Stotram is complete.
